title: Eleutheranthera tenella
taxonomy: Eleutheranthera tenella is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Eleutheranthera, which contains plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant's classification is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, phylum - Magnoliophyta, class - Magnoliopsida, order - Asterales, family - Asteraceae, genus - Eleutheranthera, species - E. tenella. Eleutheranthera tenella is closely related to other members of the Asteraceae family. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and genetic makeup.
uses: Eleutheranthera tenella has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world for its purported health benefits. intro: Eleutheranthera tenella, also known as "barbatimão", is a plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include the potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, as well as exhibit antioxidant and anti-diabetic activities. The bioactive constituents of Eleutheranthera tenella include flavonoids, triterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
